title: Concordia Reputation Attestation
description: A signed, structured record of negotiation behavior and outcome. Produced automatically from every Concordia session transcript. Attestations are the raw material of trust — they record what happened without computing scores. Scoring is performed by external reputation services that consume attestations.

concordia_attestation.description: Attestation schema version (semver). Matches the protocol version.

attestation_id.description: Unique identifier for this attestation. Recommended format: 'att_' followed by a UUID v4.

session_id.description: The negotiation session this attestation records.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of attestation generation.

duration_seconds.description: Wall-clock time from session open to conclusion.

terms_count.description: Number of terms in the negotiation.

resolution_mechanism.description: How agreement was reached (or 'none' if not reached).

parties.description: Behavioral record for each party in the negotiation.

role.description: The agent's role in this negotiation.

behavior.description: Quantified behavioral signals derived from the transcript.

offers_made.description: Number of offers and counteroffers submitted.

concessions.description: Number of times the agent moved toward the counterparty's position.

concession_magnitude.description: Average concession size as a fraction of the term's range. 0.0 = no movement; 1.0 = full concession to counterparty's position.

signals_shared.description: Number of voluntary preference signals shared (§3.4).

constraints_violated.description: Number of own constraints the agent later contradicted. A strong indicator of bad-faith negotiation.

reasoning_provided.description: Whether the agent used the reasoning field in any message.

withdrawal.description: Whether the agent withdrew from the negotiation.

response_time_avg_seconds.description: Average time to respond to counterparty messages.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over this party's behavioral record, confirming accuracy.

meta.description: Contextual metadata. Reveals the type of negotiation without exposing deal specifics.

category.description: Dot-separated category (e.g., 'electronics.cameras'). MAY be omitted for privacy.

value_range.description: Logarithmic value bucket (e.g., '1000-5000_USD'). Preserves privacy while enabling size-weighted scoring.

extensions_used.description: Protocol extensions active in this session.

transcript_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the complete negotiation transcript. Allows independent verification.

fulfillment.description: Post-settlement fulfillment record. Null until settlement is complete. Updated after delivery/service completion.

fulfillment_attestation.description: Records whether the agreed terms were actually honored after settlement.

fulfilled_at.description: When fulfillment was confirmed. Null if not yet fulfilled.

description.description: Brief description of the dispute. No deal specifics — just the nature of the issue.

disputes.description: Disputes raised about specific terms. Empty array if no disputes.

counterparty_attestation.description: The counterparty's confirmation of fulfillment. Mutual attestation creates stronger trust signals.

notes.description: Optional brief note about the experience.

reputation_query.description: Standard format for querying an agent's reputation from a reputation service.

reputation_response.description: Standard response format from a reputation service. The protocol defines the shape; services define the scoring.

service_id.description: Identifier of the reputation service providing this response.

confidence.description: How confident the service is in this score. Low confidence = few attestations.

agreement_rate.description: Fraction of negotiations that reached agreement.

fulfillment_rate.description: Fraction of agreements that were fulfilled.

reasoning_rate.description: Fraction of negotiations where reasoning was provided.

median_rounds_to_agreement.description: Median number of rounds in successful negotiations.

categories_active.description: Categories this agent has negotiated in.

context_specific.description: Scores filtered to the query context (category, value range, role).

flags.description: Notable flags. Both positive and negative signals.

service_signature.description: Ed25519 signature from the reputation service, creating accountability for the scoring.
